description: Island nation known for advanced technology, traditional culture, and unique blend of ancient and modern. Famous for cherry blossoms, anime, and efficient public transportation.
notable_landmarks: Mount Fuji, Tokyo Tower, Kyoto temples, Hiroshima Peace Memorial
cultural_highlights: Tea ceremonies, sumo wrestling, anime and manga culture, traditional festivals like Hanami
historical_facts: Isolated from world for 200+ years during Edo period, rapid modernization during Meiji Restoration, economic miracle after WWII

description: Largest country in South America, home to Amazon rainforest and vibrant culture. Known for Carnival, football, and diverse ecosystems from beaches to rainforests.
notable_landmarks: Christ the Redeemer statue, Amazon rainforest, Iguazu Falls, Copacabana beach
cultural_highlights: Carnival festival, samba music and dance, capoeira martial art, diverse ethnic cuisine
historical_facts: Portuguese colonization began 1500, independence in 1822, capital moved from Rio to Brasília in 1960

description: Scandinavian country famous for fjords, northern lights, and high quality of life. Pioneer in environmental sustainability and social welfare programs.
notable_landmarks: Geirangerfjord, Bergen's Bryggen wharf, Northern Lights viewing spots
cultural_highlights: Viking heritage, outdoor culture including skiing, midnight sun in summer, strong environmental consciousness
historical_facts: Viking Age exploration and settlements, union with Denmark then Sweden, independence in 1905

description: Ancient civilization cradle with iconic pyramids and pharaonic history. Modern nation at crossroads of Africa and Middle East, home to Suez Canal.
notable_landmarks: Great Pyramids of Giza, Sphinx, Valley of the Kings, Karnak Temple complex
cultural_highlights: Ancient hieroglyphics and art, belly dancing, Arabic calligraphy, spice markets and bazaars
historical_facts: One of world's oldest civilizations dating 3000 BCE, ruled by pharaohs for millennia, British protectorate until 1952

description: Second-largest country by area, known for natural beauty, multiculturalism, and friendly people. Features diverse landscapes from Arctic tundra to temperate rainforests.
notable_landmarks: Niagara Falls, CN Tower, Rocky Mountains, Parliament Hill
cultural_highlights: Ice hockey culture, maple syrup production, Indigenous First Nations art, bilingual heritage
historical_facts: Indigenous peoples for thousands of years, French and British colonization, confederation in 1867

description: Technological powerhouse with rich cultural heritage. Known for K-pop, cuisine, and rapid economic development. Divided from North Korea since 1953.
notable_landmarks: Gyeongbokgung Palace, DMZ border, Jeju Island, Bukchon Hanok Village
cultural_highlights: K-pop and K-dramas, kimchi and Korean BBQ, taekwondo martial art, respect for elders tradition
historical_facts: Ancient kingdoms unified in 7th century, Japanese occupation 1910-1945, Korean War 1950-1953, rapid industrialization

description: East African nation famous for wildlife safaris and diverse ecosystems. Home to Maasai people and birthplace of humanity based on fossil discoveries.
notable_landmarks: Maasai Mara game reserve, Mount Kenya, Amboseli National Park, Great Rift Valley
cultural_highlights: Maasai warrior culture, traditional beadwork, safari tourism, long-distance running excellence
historical_facts: Cradle of humankind with fossil discoveries, British colonial period, independence in 1963

description: Island continent with unique wildlife, beaches, and Outback desert. Known for laid-back culture and dangerous creatures. Indigenous Aboriginal culture dates back 65000+ years.
notable_landmarks: Sydney Opera House, Great Barrier Reef, Uluru (Ayers Rock), Bondi Beach
cultural_highlights: Surfing culture, Aboriginal art and dreamtime stories, barbecue lifestyle, unique wildlife like kangaroos
historical_facts: Indigenous Aboriginal peoples oldest continuous culture, British penal colony from 1788, federation in 1901

description: Ancient Mesoamerican civilizations met Spanish colonization creating vibrant mestizo culture. Known for cuisine, Day of the Dead celebrations, and archaeological sites.
notable_landmarks: Chichen Itza pyramid, Teotihuacan, Copper Canyon, Mayan ruins at Tulum
cultural_highlights: Mariachi music, Day of the Dead festival, taco and mole cuisine, lucha libre wrestling
historical_facts: Aztec and Mayan civilizations, Spanish conquest 1521, independence in 1821, Mexican Revolution 1910-1920

description: Low-lying country with extensive canal systems and dike engineering. Known for windmills, tulips, liberal social policies, and cycling culture.
notable_landmarks: Keukenhof tulip gardens, Anne Frank House, windmills at Kinderdijk, Van Gogh Museum
cultural_highlights: Cycling culture, cheese and stroopwafel treats, Golden Age art masters, tolerance and progressive policies
historical_facts: Golden Age of trade and art in 17th century, land reclamation from sea, neutral in WWI, occupied in WWII

description: World's most populous democracy with incredible diversity of languages, religions, and cultures. Ancient civilization with modern tech industry. Known for spices, yoga, and Bollywood.
notable_landmarks: Taj Mahal, Himalayan mountains, Ganges River, Amber Fort
cultural_highlights: Yoga and meditation, Bollywood cinema, diverse cuisine with spices, Holi festival of colors
historical_facts: Indus Valley Civilization 3300 BCE, Mughal Empire, British Raj colonization, independence in 1947

description: Island nation in South Pacific with stunning landscapes featured in Lord of the Rings films. Known for Māori culture, adventure sports, and kiwi birds.
notable_landmarks: Fiordland National Park, Hobbiton movie set, Rotorua geothermal areas, Milford Sound
cultural_highlights: Māori haka dance and culture, rugby passion, adventure sports like bungee jumping, conservation efforts
historical_facts: Māori settlement from Polynesia 1250-1300, British colonization from 1840, Treaty of Waitangi
